title: The Psychological Toll of the Modern Metropolis
description: For questions 17 – 24, read the text below. Use the word given in capitals at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: Early sociologists did not always (0) envisage the profound psychological shifts triggered by the relentless pace of rapid urbanization. As populations migrated from rural heartlands to burgeoning metropolises, a noticeable (17) toward social alienation emerged among the workforce. To (18) the physiological stressors inherent in high-density living, modern urban planners now prioritize the integration of 'green pockets.' However, for many city (19) , the constant (20) of the senses remains a significant barrier to achieving mental equilibrium. || Standing amidst towering glass structures, an individual may frequently experience a crushing sense of their own (21) This existential unease is often exacerbated by the perceived (22) of traditional community ties and ancestral support networks. Despite these psychological hurdles, the (23) of urban infrastructure and economic opportunity continues to exert a powerful pull. Consequently, the social fabric of the city must be (24) through more empathetic and human-centric architectural design.

example.text: Early sociologists did not always (0) ENVISAGE (VISION)

questions.explanation: Requires a noun to follow the adjective 'noticeable'.
Requires a verb meaning to balance or counteract; formed by adding a prefix.
Requires a plural agent noun referring to people living in a place.
Requires a noun with a prefix indicating 'too much' to fit the context of urban stress.
Requires a noun with a negative prefix to denote a lack of importance.
Requires a plural noun; involves an internal vowel change from the base verb.
Requires an abstract noun indicating the quality of being dependable.
Requires a passive verb form involving an internal vowel change and a suffix.
